'''Ben Cockayne''' (born 20 July 1983) is an English former [[rugby league]] footballer who usually played as a {{rlp|fb}} or on the {{rlp|wg}}. He is most remembered for his two spells with [[Hull Kingston Rovers]], with whom he won two promotions to the [[Super League]], in 2006 and 2017, respectively. He also played for [[Hunslet Hawks]], [[Doncaster RLFC]],  [[Featherstone Rovers]], [[Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]] and [[York City Knights]].

==Background==
Cockayne was born in [[Pontefract]], [[West Yorkshire]], England.<ref name="hkr"/>

==Career==
===Doncaster===
Ben started his [[rugby league]] career with his local amateur club [[Normanton (rugby league)|Normanton Knights]]. He had some trials with Super League sides such as [[Wakefield Trinity]] and [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]]. He was then given a chance to play for [[Doncaster R.L.F.C.|Doncaster]] in [[RFL Championship|National League One]] on a trial basis by [[St. John Ellis]] who was then the coach of Doncaster.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2006/jul/18/rugbyleague.comment|title=Scouting report: Ben Cockayne, Hull Kingston Rovers|work=The Guardian|first=John|last=Kear|date=18 July 2006|accessdate=6 December 2016}}</ref>

===Hull Kingston Rovers===
After impressing for Doncaster, especially against Hull Kingston Rovers in 2005 at Craven Park, Cockayne signed for [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] whilst they were in National League One. During the 2006 season, Cockayne was a first team regular in the squad, playing 29 games and helping his side to promotion by scoring 27 tries. He came second in the ranks for top try-scorer for Hull Kingston Rovers in 2006, and was ''Man-of-the-Match'' in the Robins' memorable game that saw them be promoted to Super League.

In the 2006 season, Hull Kingston Rovers won the Rugby League National League One competition and were promoted to the Super League competition for the 2007 season.<ref name="The inside story of Hull KR's 2006 Grand Final win as told by key players">{{cite web |title=The inside story of Hull KR's 2006 Grand Final win as told by key players |url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/hull-kr-2006-grand-final-3989547 |website=Hull Daily Mail |accessdate=26 May 2020}}</ref>

[[File:Ben Cockayne.JPG|thumb|right|Cockayne playing for Hull KR]]
He left in July 2011 following a suspension for a number of off-field incidents.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/14168170|title=Hull KR and suspended Ben Cockayne part company|work=BBC|date=15 July 2011|accessdate=6 December 2016}}</ref>

===Featherstone Rovers===
The departure from Hull KR came just after the closure of the Super League signing deadline, so Cockayne signed for Championship side [[Featherstone Rovers]] until the end of the season.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/14219029|title=Featherstone Rovers sign ex-Hull KR back Ben Cockayne|work=BBC|date=20 July 2011|accessdate=6 December 2016}}</ref> On 2 October 2011 Featherstone won the [[Championship Grand Final]], and later that month Cockayne signed with Wakefield Trinity.<ref name="BBC10oct2011">{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/15243291|title=Ben Cockayne pens Wakefield Trinity Wildcats contract|work=BBC|date=10 October 2011|accessdate=6 December 2016}}</ref>

===Wakefield Trinity===
Cockayne initially signed a one-year contract<ref name="BBC10oct2011"/> with Wakefield which was extended with a two-year deal<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/17581947|title=Wakefield Wildcats' Ben Cockayne gets new two-year deal|work=BBC|date=2 April 2012|accessdate=6 December 2016}}</ref> and he went on to play 45 times for Trinity. However, in 2013 Wakefield Trinity were forced to sell players to pay off debts and Cockayne moved back to Hull Kingston Rovers at the end of 2013.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/23975276|title=Wakefield: Ben Cockayne set to re-join Hull KR|work=BBC|date=5 September 2013|accessdate=6 December 2016}}</ref>

===Hull Kingston Rovers===
In the 2014 season, Cockayne transferred to Hull Kingston Rovers, where he replaced Eden as Rovers' regular Full Back. Regarded by many as a terrace hero during his first stint at Hull Kingston Rovers, he became a fans' favourite once again due to his form during his second stint.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/EXCLUSIVE-Hull-KR-want-Ben-Cockayne-Wakefield/story-19464324-detail/story.html|title=EXCLUSIVE: Hull KR want Ben Cockayne back from Wakefield Wildcats|date=3 July 2013|newspaper=Hull Daily Mail|accessdate=18 February 2017|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140525213930/http://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/EXCLUSIVE-Hull-KR-want-Ben-Cockayne-Wakefield/story-19464324-detail/story.html|archive-date=25 May 2014|url-status=dead}}</ref>

===York City Knights===
In September 2017 it was announced that Cockayne had signed to play for York City Knights in the 2018 season.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.yorkpress.co.uk/sport/15509864.Another_York_City_Knights_coup___Hull_KR_star_Ben_Cockayne_signs_for_2018/ |title=Another York City Knights coup - Hull KR star Ben Cockayne signs for 2018 |work=[[The Press (York)|The Press]] |date=2 September 2017 |accessdate=1 December 2017}}</ref>

==Controversy==
In 2009 Cockayne was found guilty of actual bodily harm after CCTV footage showed him punching and kicking a man in the head. Cockayne was given a 51 week jail sentence suspended for 2 years. He was also ordered to complete 240 hours of community service.<ref name="Rugby star attacker avoids jail">{{cite web|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/1/hi/england/humber/7979664.stm|title=Rugby star attacker avoids jail|website=BBC News|date=2 April 2009|access-date=25 May 2020}}</ref>

In April 2011, Cockayne allegedly posted racist comments on Facebook. Cockayne's Facebook profile was removed within hours of the Mirror newspaper contacting his club and the Rugby Football League. A Hull Kingston Rovers spokesman said: "The club will be carrying out a full internal investigation into comments allegedly made on a website by Ben Cockayne. Nobody will be commenting further until that has concluded." The Rugby Football League, which has a Kick Racism into Touch campaign, said: "We will be speaking to Hull Kingston Rovers about a potential social media matter."<ref>[https://www.mirror.co.uk/news/technology/2011/04/21/rugby-league-star-ben-cockayne-faces-probe-over-alleged-racist-comments-on-facebook-115875-23075799/#ixzz1KFe0eT2C Rugby League star Ben Cockayne faces probe over alleged racist comments on Facebook]. mirror.co.uk (21 April 2011). Retrieved on 11 October 2011.</ref> Cockayne was fined by Hull KR, but faced no further punishment from the police after dropping their own investigation of the incident.<ref>{{cite web|last1=Wilson|first1=Andy|title=Hull KR fine Ben Cockayne after police drop racism investigation|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2011/may/12/hull-kr-fined-ben-cockayne|website=The Guardian|accessdate=14 November 2016|date=12 May 2011}}</ref>

==Retirement==
In June 2019, Cockayne called time on his rugby playing career at the age of 35,<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=29 June 2019|accessdate=4 May 2021|title=Ben Cockayne hangs up his boots|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/ben-cockayne-hangs-up-his-boots/}}</ref> but came out of retirement two years later to sign for [[Doncaster R.L.F.C.]] in [[RFL League 1]].<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=16 August 2021|accessdate=31 December 2021|title=Ben Cockayne makes rugby league return with Doncaster|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/ben-cockayne-makes-rugby-league-return-with-doncaster/}}</ref> In July 2022, he left Doncaster to take up a coaching role at [[York City Knights]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Knights name former player Cockayne as new assistant coach |url=https://www.yorkpress.co.uk/sport/20285988.knights-name-former-player-cockayne-new-assistant-coach/ |website=York Press |access-date=7 September 2023 |date=18 July 2022}}</ref>
